LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN MATEO UNION HIGH SCHOOLD DISTRICT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- DEFENDANT SAN MATEO UNION HIGH SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION -- GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: 1. PLAINTIFF'S BID WAS NON-RESPONSIVE AND CONTRACT WAS NOT FORMAL; 2. THE CONDITIONAL BID ACCEPTANCE PERMITTED, SEE THE TAYLOR CASE; 3. NO ESTOPPEL CLAIM - RELIANCE UNREASONABLE, IN VIEW OF BID BEING NON-RESPONSIVE, ALSO SEE UNIVERSAL BY-PRODUCT INC. CASE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND ORDER AND COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF CRC 391. JUDGE: RONALD E.QUIDACHAY. REPORTER: BELDON YEE, CSR # 2647

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-06-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEAR: John A. Shupe, Esq. of SHUPE AND FINKELSTEIN 650/341-3693 for defendant SAN MATEO
UNION HIGH SCHOOL DISTRICT and moving for summary adjudication of the 1st and 2nd Causes of Action
Alexander Bannon, Esq. of McINERNEY & DILLON 510/4657100 for Plaintiff and opposing
party
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows -- DEFENDANT SAN MATEO UNION HIGH SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION -- Grant motion for summary judgment: 1. plaintiff's bid was non-responsive and contract was not formal; 2. The conditional bid acceptance permitted, see the Taylor case; 3. no estoppel claim - reliance unreasonable, in view of bid being non-responsive, also see Universal By-Product Inc. case. Prevailing party is to prepare and order and comply with the provisions of CRC 391. (302/REQ/PB) REPORTER: BELDON YEE CSR #2647

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-19-2005 Off Calendar

MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar Jun-03-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories
The Court finds that the motion is timely pursuant to CCP Section 2033(l).
The motion is granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. plaintiff is ordered to provide complete verified responses within 15 days of notice of this order. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anita Remes, Esq. appearing for defendant/opposing party San Mateo Union High School District

RULING:
After hearing, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiff's motion for a protective order to limit the deposition of the person most knowledgeable to one 1/2 day session in the afternoon is denied. Deposition set for Jun 2, 2005, one day. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
